entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
For dynpro, you may use the debugger, you may switch any debugger tool with Special tool > Dynpro analysis, it shows the hierarchy of dynpros, subscreens, controls, etc. I would appreciate that you share a screenshot of this hierarchy (EDIT: showing program names and dynpro numbers).

e.g. Dynpro Analysis of ME23N > Item > Delivery Schedule:

entry.title: Re: Use of Global Accounting Hierarchies and Matrix Consolidation in Consolidation Reports
entry.content:
Hi

We would like to use Matrix consolidation for the management reporting component of our Group Reporting implementation, i.e. we require eliminations between profit centers. My query is how elimination works at PL30. Do you have any example to view the COI data in case of profit center or segment wise reporting?

entry.title: Low throughput in SAP HANA, IBM POWER
entry.content:
We faced a problem with low throughput in SAP HANA, measured in statements per second executed by sql_executor threads.

Metrics: The current productive system runs on Oracle 19.24, where we can achieve throughput of 800,000 user calls per second. We consider Oracle user call per sec and HANA statements per sec to be equivalent. Additionally, throughput can be monitored by the number of network packets per second at the OS level (for example, using nmon).

Test description: The test is multi-threaded. From a client host running a Python script, we execute queries like SELECT ID FROM PYTHON_MON.ZBC_TEST_N_<from 0001 to 00230> to one of the HANA nodes. Each query always returns one row; each table contains one row. The number of tables used in the test varies from 120 to 1500. The issue becomes noticeable already at 150 active threads. The HANA node is a P10 server with 16 NUMA nodes, 32TB RAM, SMT8 (we also tested SMT2 and SMT4). In this configuration, SAP HANA can reach about 100,000 statements/sec. Tests were also done with real data in ABAP, selecting from tables like MARA, MARC – the throughput was also unsatisfactory.

Parameters that positively affect throughput:
composite_statistics_striping=numa
resource_tracking=false
statistics_type=7
Setting these parameters on an empty database without much data loaded can improve performance by 2–2.5 times.

Hardware throughput test:
We tried creating up to 16 index servers on the HANA node,
binding CPU cores to a NUMA node.
Example: ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('daemon.ini','SYSTEM') SET ('indexserver.H49', 'instance_affinity[3]')='480-719';
All test tables were located on their respective index servers, and the load from clients was sent to their assigned index servers.
In this configuration, a single HANA node on an IBM Power P10 server delivered a total throughput of 4,200,000 stm/sec across all index servers.

Query execution speed in a multi-threaded test:
Average selection speed was checked with the script "HANA_Connections_Statistics_2.00.060+.txt"
fields AVG_SEL_MS+AVG_PREP_MS.
In Oracle, we used a script based on SQL cache or AWR:
select Round((elapsed_time/DECODE(executions ,0,1,executions))/1000000,4) avg_elap_sec,s.* from v$sql s where s.SQL_ID = &sqlid.
When SAP HANA’s overall throughput degrades, the speed of individual queries does not decrease. The load on SAP HANA does not scale. No matter how many client connections are opened, only about 100–120 active threads on the server are doing useful work.

 

What we tried from SAP Notes:
We checked all available notes related to the issue.
For configuration we used:
"HANA_Configuration_MiniChecks_2.00.080+.txt"
"HANA_Threads_Callstacks_MiniChecks_2.00.060+.txt"
"HANA_Configuration_Parameters_2.00.040+.txt" and others.
We analyzed RTE dumps and perf traces at the OS level.
More than 500 SAP HANA parameters recommended in SAP Notes were tested.

SAP HANA releases:
The issue exists on all versions we tested.
Current version – 2.00.081.00.1733303410 SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP6 6.4.0-150600.23.25-default
We tested on 79, 78, 37 with different SPs.
Servers: IBM Power P9 and P10.
Operating systems: SLES, RHEL.

entry.title: BI2025: Side-by-Side Migration vs. In-Place Update.
entry.content:
Updating your BIP is a critical decision that impacts business continuity, performance, and cost. Two primary approaches exist to update BIP to latest version: side-by-side migration and in-place update. Understanding the nuances of each method is essential for making the best choice for your specific needs. This article explores the key differences, advantages, and disadvantages of both strategies to help you determine the optimal path for your organization and your IT Infrastructure

There were some changes in SAP maintenance strategy for BIP so please refer to updated SAP BusinessObjects Maintenance Strategy 2024 document.

 

Choosing between an in-place upgrade of Business Intelligence Platform and a side-by-side migration to a newer 2025 version involves several considerations. Each has its advantages and disadvantages:

In-Place Update:

Side-by-side migration:

 

  • Pros:
  1. Lower initial cost: Generally less expensive upfront as it doesn't involve new hardware or parallel infrastructure.
  2. Less disruption (potentially): If successful, minimizes downtime and impact on existing reports and users.
  3. Simplified process: Fewer moving parts compared to a side-by-side migration.
  • Cons:
  1. Higher risk: Potential for complications during the upgrade process, leading to extended downtime and data loss if not planned and executed meticulously.
  2. Limited testing opportunities: Difficult to thoroughly test the upgraded environment without impacting production.
  3. Forced upgrades: May necessitate upgrading underlying operating systems, databases, or other components, adding complexity. Check PAM for BI2025
  4. No fallback option: If the upgrade fails, reverting can be complex and time-consuming.
  5. Technical debt: Perpetuates older technology, delaying the inevitable move to a modern platform.

 

 

 

  • Pros:
  1. Lower risk: Allows thorough testing and validation in a separate environment before switching over, minimizing disruption to production.
  2. Flexibility: Provides the opportunity to re-evaluate existing reports and processes, optimize data models, and leverage new platform features.
  3. Fallback option: If issues arise during the migration, you can easily revert to the existing BI4 environment.
  4. Enables phased rollout: Allows migrating reports and users incrementally, reducing the impact of change.
  5. Access to new features: Take full advantage of the latest technologies and capabilities of the new BI platform.
  • Cons:
  1. Higher initial cost: Requires investment in new hardware, software, and potentially consulting services.
  2. More complex process: Involves more steps and coordination compared to an in-place upgrade.
  3. Data migration challenges: Requires careful planning and execution to ensure data integrity and consistency during migration.
  4. Dual maintenance: Requires maintaining both the old and new environments until the migration is complete.

 

In-Place Upgrade vs. Side-by-side migration.

Key Decision Factors:

  • Complexity of the existing BI4 environment: A more complex environment with numerous reports, users, and integrations generally favors a side-by-side approach.
  • Budget and resources: In-place upgrades are generally less expensive upfront but can become costly if complications arise.
  • Risk tolerance: Side-by-side migration offers a lower-risk approach, especially for mission-critical BI systems.
  • Desired future state: If the goal is to modernize and optimize the BI platform, side-by-side migration is the preferred option.
  • Time constraints: In-place upgrades can be quicker if successful, but side-by-side migrations allow for phased rollouts and better control over timelines.

Recommendation:

In most cases, a side-by-side migration is the recommended approach for moving from BI4 to a newer BI2025 platform. While more complex and initially costly, it offers significantly lower risk, greater flexibility, and the opportunity to modernize the BI environment fully. An in-place upgrade should only be considered in very limited scenarios where cost is the absolute primary driver and the existing BI4 environment is relatively simple. Even then, a thorough risk assessment is crucial.

entry.title: SAP Business Transformation Center is Now part of the Integrated Toolchain powered by SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
I am excited to announce the latest enhancement to our SAP Business Transformation Center: The integration of Lean Selective Data Transition with SAP CALM Project & Task Management. This powerful combination is designed to provide comprehensive transition guidance, ensuring a smooth and efficient migration from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA.

Why Lean Selective Data Transition? 

The Lean Selective Data Transition scenario offers a unique approach to data migration, allowing organizations to leave behind outdated company code or fiscal year data and focus on the most relevant information. This capability not only reduces the data scope but also enhances the efficiency and speed of the migration process. The transformation project implementation efforts is reduced significantly, since data scoping, modeling as no-code automated setup of the migration implementation and execution of the migration cycle can be centrally controlled in SAP Business Transformation Center and the end to end setup happens in minutes. Further information can be found here.  

Integration with SAP CALM Project & Task Management 

With the new integration, SAP Business Transformation Center that is also running on SAP Cloud ALM is now fully connected to SAP Cloud ALMs Project & Task Management and part of the Integrated Toolchain. This feature allows the creation of a SAP Cloud ALM project with reference to an SAP Business Transformation Centers 'Transformation Project', containing all transformation project-related tasks. This guided procedure generates immense value in user experience by navigating through the entire end-to-end process with direct navigation to apps on SAP Cloud ALM using native SAP Cloud ALM Project & Task Management capabilities. The first version of the new app comes with guidance for the transition approach 'Selective Data Transition'. Stay tuned for upcoming further supported scenarios. 

Key Features and Benefits 

  • Comprehensive Transition Guidance: The new app "Manage Transformation Projects" and the key element "Transformation Project" provide detailed guidance for selective data transition from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA. 
  • Enhanced User Experience: The integration offers a seamless navigation experience, guiding users through the entire process with direct links to relevant apps on SAP Cloud ALM. 
  • Customizable Tasks: Users can create manual tasks to extend the standard guided procedure with custom project-specific requirements, tailoring the transformation projects to their needs. 
  • Efficient Data Scope Reduction: The Lean Selective Data Transition scenario supports time-slice filtering, allowing organizations to selectively transition data based on specific key dates. 
  • Accelerated Project Execution: The integration with SAP Cloud ALM accelerates and simplifies the project through a work-breakdown structure for relevant tasks from system setup to final data migration. 

The new app "Manage Transformation Projects" 

The tile "Manage Transformation Projects" on the SAP Cloud ALM "Transformation can be used to create a Transformation Project: 

  

Transformation Page in SAP Cloud ALM 

 

In order to get the access, make sure you have assigned the respective roles "Transformation Project Administrator" to your user in the SAP Cloud ALM app "User Management": 

New Roles for the new app "Manage Transformation Projects" 

 The creation of a Transformation Project is currently only possible for the scenario "Selective Data Transition SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA".  

Creation of a new "Transformation Project" 

The SAP Cloud ALM Project in Project & Task Management will be automatically generated with the task list for the scenario "Selective Data Transition SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA".  

Created "Transformation Project" with direct link to the SAP Cloud ALM Project 

 Thanks to bi-directional integration, you can navigate from there into the "Projects and Setup App" and also back into the "Transformation Project" again:  

Projects & Setup app

The "Selective Data Transition SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA" is split into 4 deliverables that contain the respective tasks: 

Deliverables of a "Transformation Project" 

 From there it's possible to navigate into the "Tasks" app to verify all the tasks and also navigate into the tasks detail: 

Task List of a "Transformation Project" for the Lean Selective Data Transition Scenario 

 From here users will find all the mandatory tasks in order to execute a Lean Selective Data Transition. From the tasks users find guidance and links to FAQs and the documentation and it's also possible to navigate directly to the related apps, like the "Manage Digital Blueprints" app, which is the app to execute the task "Create Digital Blueprint". 

Direct Navigation from SAP Project & Task Management into other apps, such as SAP Business Transformation Center

Using the task list, users and customers are guided end to end through their transformation project journey. 

Seamless Integration with Digital Blueprint

The Selective Data Transition scenario can be utilized using the Digital Blueprint in the app "Manage Digital Blueprints". The Transformation Project can be linked to the Digital Blueprint during creation. The Digital Blueprint provides a virtual representation of your source SAP ECC system, helping you define the migration and transformation scope with precision. The relevant time-slice filtering is then incorporated into the content of the transformation objects in the transformation model, ensuring a streamlined and efficient transition. 

Create a Digital Blueprint with reference to a Transformation Project

 

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

The integration of Lean Selective Data Transition with SAP Cloud ALMs Project & Task Management is a game-changer for organizations looking to migrate from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA. By leveraging this powerful toolchain, you can achieve a seamless and guided transition, reduce data scope, and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements. Embrace the future of data migration with SAP Business Transformation Center and unlock the full potential of your digital transformation journey.

entry.title: CPQ Reporting Module - Query Usage Question
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

 

We would like to use the reporting module available in CPQ, specifically to build data sources using the “Query” type, which allows custom SQL queries for data selection. We’d like to confirm that when using SQL queries in the reporting module, only SELECT operations are allowed, and any SQL operations that modify data or table structures are prohibited on the system level.

Could you please confirm this? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CPQ Reporting Module - Query Usage Question
entry.content:
Yes, your understanding is absolutely correct. In the SAP CPQ Reporting Module, when creating custom data sources using the "Query" type, only read-only SQL operations (SELECT) are supported. The system strictly prohibits any SQL commands that modify data or database structures—such as INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, ALTER, etc.—to ensure data integrity and security.

The reporting module is designed specifically for data retrieval, supporting features like filtering, grouping, and aggregations. This read-only approach aligns with best practices and helps safeguard against unauthorized data manipulation or structural changes.

entry.title: Re: End of Maintenance for Basic Authentication
entry.content: @PL1The customer is asking why it says the depreciation date is Nov 1st instead of Nov 13th on the log in notice. Where can we request to fix the date that they see?

entry.title: Re: Where can I start learning features and functions of SAP Sustainability Data Exchange?

entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Journal Entry Item - Read (A2X) to read journal entry items and detailed company code, cost center, and profit center information using this inbound service.

Link for Business Accelerator Hub

Regards,

Bhawesh

entry.title: Re: Analytical Query CDS View creation and consumption in RSRT along with publishing OData Services
entry.content:
Hello,

The new one is transient view entity ( from 7.56 or 7.58 is not clear).

And it can be exposed with InA protocol other than Odata, which is a little faster.

entry.title: Re: BTP Neo to Cloud Foundry Migration

entry.title: Seamless Planning with Datasphere & SAC: Seamless Planning in the Cloud?
entry.content:
What is Seamless Planning? 

Seamless Planning creates a tighter integration between planning in SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. This integration makes it possible to create planning models in SAC, while the associated transaction and master data is stored in SAP Datasphere.  

The model definition and calculation logic remain within SAC, while the persistence for master data and planning data takes place in SAP Datasphere, so that both systems are closely linked - without the effort of manual data transfers or imports. 

From BI-directional to Seamless Planning

Previously, planning data had to be stored in the SAC and then regularly exported to external data warehouse systems. Although it was possible, for example, to use planning data via virtual tables in SAP Datasphere, this required SAP Cloud Data Integration as an additional component. Seamless Planning eliminates this additional step for new models. The data remains synchronized throughout and is available in real time for both planning and further analyses in Datasphere. However, a direct migration of existing classic SAC models is not yet possible, but has been announced. 

Seamless Planning is not a separate piece of software, but a change to the persistence of planning models in SAC. The aim of this development is to provide companies with a more flexible, scalable and reliable planning solution that is directly linked to their data warehouse. 

Seamless Planning functions

Seamless Planning offers a range of functions that optimize the integration between the SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. 

  • Automatic data synchronization: Changes to transaction and master data in the SAC are automatically transferred to SAP Datasphere, increasing data consistency and eliminating the need for manual exports.
  • Real-time data availability: By storing the data in SAP Datasphere, it is available in real time for analysis purposes, which speeds up decision-making.
  • Extended usage options: SAC models and objects can be used in SAP Datasphere, for example in Transformation Flows or Analytic Models, making the integration and analysis of data more flexible. This is possible with Seamless Planning without SAP Cloud Data Integration.
  • Reduction of manual activities: Direct access to planning data in SAP Datasphere reduces the need for manual exports and imports, making the planning process more efficient. 
  • Centralized data storage: By storing planning and analysis data in a common database, data redundancies are reduced and a harmonized data landscape is created. 

These functions help to simplify planning processes, promote collaboration between different business areas and enable well-founded decisions based on consistent data. 

Prerequisites for the use of Seamless Planning 

  • Technical requirements: SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and SAP Datasphere must be operated in the same data center. There must be a 1:1 relationship between the respective tenants. 
  • System requirements: The SAC tenant must run on the HANA Cloud infrastructure. This infrastructure has been used as standard for new tenants since mid-2024. 
  • Licensing: No additional license costs, provided the technical requirements are met. 

These technical requirements are a necessary condition for the use of Seamless Planning. Companies already working with SAP Analytics Cloud and Datasphere should check whether their infrastructure is compatible. It should be noted that classic SAC planning models (without measures) cannot be used in Seamless Planning. 

In addition, shared dimensions must be stored within the same SAP Datasphere Space in order to be usable in multiple planning models. 

Process flow and required steps

Current status and SAP roadmap 

In the fourth quarter of 2024, Seamless Planning was introduced in the Controlled Release for registered customers and partners; since the Q1.2025 release, Seamless Planning has been generally available - for all customers who meet the above requirements and link both Tennants for Seamless Planning. With this initial release, master and plan data can be saved in Datasphere as described and used there in views, analytic models and transformation flows - but initially only as a source; the content of the plan data tables generated from SAC is “protected” in Datasphere and can only be modified via SAC, but not directly in Datasphere. 

When using existing content, e.g. from SAP, it is possible to select where the deployment (of the persistence layer) should take place when using seamless planning. 

Future developments include the integration of existing analytical models, the reuse of SAP Datasphere dimensions in SAC planning models and the improvement of the authorization concept. This will soon open up functions that go far beyond the current scope of planning with SAC and Datasphere. 

From the later SAC releases in 2025, Seamless Planning will offer numerous new options that will enable deeper integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. The most important enhancements include: 

  • Improved integration of existing data models: Creation of SAC planning models with seamless planning based on existing Datasphere models. 
  • Display of parent/child hierarchies: The display of hierarchical structures in SAP Datasphere is optimized to better represent such relationships.
  • Make local dimensions visible: Local dimensions can be displayed and used directly in SAP Datasphere. Currently, only public dimensions can be used, apart from the special dimensions for date and version, of course.
  • Cross-orchestration integration: Workflows from SAC (Multi Actions and Datasphere (Task Chains) are integrated so that Datasphere actions can be triggered directly from planning in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Live use of factual data: Real-time data from SAP Datasphere can be used directly in SAC planning models. This enables the simple (re)use of existing data models in planning. This can significantly simplify and accelerate data model development.
  • Cross-space object sharing: Objects can be shared across different spaces, which facilitates or even enables reuse and adaptation to customer-specific governance requirements.
  • Model migration to seamless planning: Migration of existing SAC planning models; models currently have to be newly created or transferred to the content network in order to be able to use them in seamless planning.
  • Reuse of dimensions and data access controls: Dimensions and data access controls from SAP Datasphere can be used directly in SAC models. This simplifies user management as an operational task.
  • Writing to SAC models: Data can be written directly from SAP Datasphere to the SAC model using transformation flows, which further simplifies integration. 

These enhancements promote a consistent database and enable more efficient planning and well-founded data-driven decisions. In addition, the reuse of existing data models is dramatically improved.  

Premises for planning architectures

The premises for planning architectures include essential aspects that ensure efficient and seamless planning. Only if these are fulfilled will the planning architecture meet the task of providing good and sustainable support for decision-making and maximizing the value of the planning data. Data integration plays a central role here by storing the planning data in the same systems in which master and reference data is managed. This enables a consistent database, simplified operation and reduces redundancies.  

Report integration ensures that planning and forecasting data can be easily incorporated into reports, ensuring transparent and consistent analysis and communication of key performance indicators and increasing the value of planning data.  

Finally, user integration is crucial: a unified environment that combines planning, forecasting and reporting provides users with an intuitive and efficient way of working, increases productivity and minimizes switching between different tools or platforms. These premises form the basis for a modern and efficient planning architecture and can be supported with seamless planning.  

Basic paths to seamless planning

Seamless planning already offers a powerful range of functions and represents an important step towards closer integration of planning and data management. However, the technical requirements for its use are not trivial and require careful consideration. Even though integration into SAP Datasphere is being continuously improved, there are still some relevant limitations at present. 

With Semless Planning, SAP is combining the advantages of close integration - as known from BW Integrated Planning or BPC Embedded - with the advantages that Datasphere offers, for example, in terms of openness and self-service capabilities and the modern user interface and flexibility of planning in SAP Analytics Cloud. However, the key functionalities for this are currently still on the roadmap and will be successively published in the next quarterly releases. 

However, SAP's long-term vision is clear: Seamless Planning is set to become the standard architecture for modern corporate planning, particularly as a result of the Business Data Cloud and the announced Data Products and Insight Apps with planning functions. If DataProducts and Insight Apps establish themselves successfully, SAP will create a solution with benefits that go far beyond the advantages of previous (BW-based) solution architectures - including expected project runtimes, self-service options and the use of machine learning using SAP Databricks, for example for predictive forecasting use cases.  

Nevertheless, companies should not rush to postpone ongoing or planned projects, but rather carry out a strategic assessment. 

Seamless planning is above all a future-oriented architecture option that should be continuously monitored with a view to the roadmap and the further expansion of functionalities. If you want to make your planning landscape modern and flexible in the long term, you should incorporate the potential of this new integration into your IT strategy at an early stage.

entry.title: Re: What's the better way of using DIN Standard for Mater...
entry.content: *push*

entry.title: Re: Batch Operation in OData V2 Adapter in SAP Cloud Platform Integration
entry.content: Hi Sana please share the steps for the OData delete batch as well in detail. when trying I am getting below error : An error occurred while processing the request. The most likely cause of this error is the formatting of your OData request payload. Please verify that the request payload format corresponds to the EDMX metadata for the Entity that you are attempting to create

entry.title: Getting error while executing FAGLGVTR
entry.content:
Hii teams 

While performing FAGLGVTR, the following error appears.

Diagnosis

The fiscal year change was not performed for company code 1000 as more than two fiscal years are open in Asset Accounting.

Procedure

First carry out a year-end closing for fiscal year 2023 in company code 1000.

When we checked the FAA_CMP, we are unable to change the “highest fiscal year” to 2025. The field is disabled. Kindly advise as to how to execute FAGLGVRT successfully.

entry.title: ESR to MDR Proxy Migration
entry.content:
I am trying to convert a proxy from ESR to MDR, the error i am getting is "Inline type cannot be migrated. Firs convert inline to global type".

If someone has already faced this issue before, please let me know what needs to be done here.

Any help is appreciated!!

entry.title: JouleManage Autodelegation
entry.content: Hi, I cannot find the help or what's new viewer regarding Joule Manage Auto-delegation, which was delivered in 1H2025 Release. Where can I find this feature information?

entry.title: Re: Out-of-the-Box Document Grounding in SAP AI Core: RAG with SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
Hello,
In step 2, I'm getting an error 400 "resource group is not provisioned for grounding".
I confirm it has the label ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding set to true.
Here is also the result of /admin/resourceGroups API

 


Any idea what I did wrong ?

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: About the short dump RAISE_EXCEPTION due to the missing catch of KEKO_NOT_FOUND, check the SAP Notes. If they don't solve, make sure that you are testing with the exact same data as in the manual run. About the dynpro, I feel that it's dynpro SAPLCKDI 4623, module FILL_COSTGRID, so it's the internal table gt_costblock. Please try searching and reading standard SAP code by yourself, it's not very difficult to find all this information.

entry.title: Re: BODS: Integrity constraint violation error with Table...
entry.content:
Dear All,

we have solved, it was an error in the dev. In the table comparison stp the comparison was done with a wrong table. For example, when loading the table 'person_data' into hana the comparison was made with the table 'person_data' of another datastore.

Regards

Luca

entry.title: Re: Input type number in combination with binding type gi...
entry.content: Did you come up with this ugly solution or did your AI tool spit this out?

entry.title: Re: Integration of Item Level Withholding Taxes of Ariba Invoice with SAP S4 HANA
entry.content: Hi @Shreyas_RM , Thank you very much for this details, we do have scenarios where header level WHT is also used, hence we cannot turn off the parameter.  by additional indicator do you mean it has to be in Invoice screen or in CIG mapping where if should disregard WHT at item level whenever header level WHT is triggered?

entry.title: Re: End of Maintenance for Basic Authentication
entry.content: Hi team, any update on this? It's not a very difficult or complicate issue, just want to know the correct date and when the date shown on login page will be correct. thank you

entry.title: Re: Unable to change Microsoft Teams SF standard logo
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your query. This is Hannah from Employee Central Team.

It is advised to report an incident to LOD-SF-PLT via SAP For Me. Platform colleugues will reach out to you and provide possible workaround.

Best regards,

Hannah

entry.title: Re: Limitation on storage locations
entry.content:
Hello @VanessaM 

The technical limitation of over 1.6M storage location per plant is inconsequential. No sane design will ever hit this limit. @jagdeepsingh83 is perfectly right - if you worry about the limitation, you need to seriously revisit your design. 

I know of a single scenario that would mandate a substantial number of storage locations. That is spare parts management where there is a requirement to manage parts issues to each technician separately. Often, storage locations are used to model technicians here. Still, the number of storage locations will not get anywhere close to the technical limit even in huge organizations. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Warehouse Robotics available for SAP S/4 HANA EWM on Public Cloud?
entry.content: Full-fledged EWM is not available in S/4HANA Public Cloud. What is available is Warehouse Management, which is a subset of EWM functionality. You can integrate S/4HANA Public Cloud Warehouse Management with EWM using Basic Integration with SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM)

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Certification for Financial Accounting

oops!
We can't seem to find the page you are looking for

entry.title: Re: Error FAA_POST214 when reversing Asset Document of Le...
entry.content:
If you have fully followed the steps of 3555206 - Error AA422 when reversing Asset Legacy Data via AB08 (*) please consider creating of a case/issue with reference to this KBA.

(*) especially checking/updating ANLA-LDT_DATE and ANLA-DT_SEQN

entry.title: While creating a CDS View in Eclipse, i get this error. how to resolve this.
Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
Hello,

We know we cannot plan for profit center in 2026 because the GLPCP table is in compatibility scope, we need to use app fiori to plan and update the ACDOCP table, but when we use for example the KP06 transaction, GLPCP table is updated, now I still wonder: can I continue to plan in the cost center with the profit center in master data updating GLPCP table?

Thank you,

entry.title: Retention Period of PDF Attachments (on Journal Entries and Supplier Invoices)
entry.content:
Hi,

Our client requested some clarity on the default retention policy in S/4 Public Cloud.
Specifically for PDF's that are attached onto Journal Entries and or Supplier Invoices

They want to know if they need to print all documents or if they can leave it within S/4. 
They need to know if the auditors can find back all the attachments within S/4 or if they should look through paperwork.

I looked around, but did not find the requested information. all help is much appreciated!

Thanks in advance
Matthias

entry.title: Re: Budget report for Cost center with G/L Account level...
entry.content:
Hi William,

Thanks for the quick reply 🙂.

Would you please guide me more on the below scenario, if you have any documentation or help guide to follow and implement with use of business content. i have tried but only Project planning Budgeting is available in business Content. 

Setup Budgeting report with
1. Cost Center with G/L Account level
2. Profit center with G/L Account Level

I appreciate your help!!.

Best Regards,

Prasad

entry.title: I want to select multiple production orders to print in SAP public cloud.
entry.content: I want to select multiple production orders to print in SAP public cloud. How can I achieve this? If configuration is required, how can I configure it? Please provide a screenshot. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: IDOC Message's Column is Different on SAP and MII
entry.content:
Hello,

I had a similar problem yesterday. For us, it was resolved by restarting the MII Java instance. 

Cheers, Alan

entry.title: How to use Support User after enabling single-sign on
entry.content:
Hi All,
We have an issue after upgrading to version 10 (FP2208). The B1 users are managed in the SLD. Their AD accounts are linked to their B1 username and that works perfectly.

Cant seem to get he support user setup. Unless you Bind a user to a windows account, you don't see the DBs in the list of the companies and cannot manually type in the DB name. 

We don't want to bind the Support user to a specific person/individual.
Is there a way to overcome this? 

Thanks in advance,
Jerusha

entry.title: Re: How we can get the information from table BPGE that e...
entry.content:
Have you checked the values of the activity field VORGA 'Budget type budgeting/planning' (value table T022 'FI-SL Activities', text table T022T)?

NB: BPGE is 'original budget', check also tables such as BPJA 'Annual' and BPBK 'Document headers' with the Line Item tables: BPEG 'overall', BPEJ 'Annual' and BPEP 'Period'

entry.title: Re: Edit button Disabled in Business Configuration app in...
entry.content: Hi, I don't seem to hove the option to choose "No Transport" in the wizard. Is that because I am not in the Cloud?

entry.title: The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: Release Strategy for Warehouse Management
entry.content:
SAP has a long-standing commitment to supporting our clients in managing their warehouse operations with outstanding adaptability and efficiency covering simple to complex requirements across industries. With LE-WM, Stock Room Management, SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), EWM for SAP S/4HANA, SAP Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud we are continuously evolving our solution portfolio to offer the right solution for every marketsegment.  

With this Blog post we would like to give an insight on the most relevant questions when it comes to release strategy, important milestones and transformation project planning for SAP’s warehouse management solutions.

What is the release strategy for EWM for SAP S/4HANA?

We are continuously enhancing EWM for SAP S/4HANA with a clear focus on the following main themes:

  • Embedded Artificial Intelligence features to increase warehouse efficiency, data-driven decision making, simplify user-experience
  • Suite integration and modularization enabling a deep integration of warehousing processes to manufacturing and production, transportation management, order management and logistics network solutions
  • Continuous extension of features and functions addressing industry needs

With Extended Warehouse Management as an integral component of SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and onPremise we are shipping a major release in a two-year cycle (since SAP S/4HANA 2023). Additionally, we release in-between major releases, three feature packs with more innovations and features along our strategic north-star.

Learn more about the SAP S/4HANA release and maintenance strategy via SAP News Center - New SAP S/4HANA Release and Maintenance Strategy to Deliver Greater Innovation and Flexibility and SAP Maintenance Phases.

We recommend reviewing the SAP Road Map Explorer to learn more about the specific Future Features and innovations we are planning with upcoming SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain for Extended Warehouse Management releases.

What release milestones are relevant for planning the transformation of SAP EWM?

As a predecessor to EWM for SAP S/4HANA, SAP EWM 9.5, 9.4 and earlier releases, are highly productive at many of our customers. We recommend to check the specific maintenance plan for SAP EWM releases via the SAP Product Availability Matrix to understand, the important timelines of end of mainstream maintenance and to understand if there is extended maintenance available.

It is important to note that not all product versions have an availability of extended maintenance and have planned end of mainstream maintenance in 2025 (e.g. SAP EWM 9.4). Learn more about the maintenance timelines of SAP EWM via SAP Note 2911449 - Updated Maintenance Timelines for SAP EWM Product Versions.

If you are evaluating transformation options, we recommend the following SAP Blog Posts for further information - Transformation to SAP S/4HANA for Warehouse and Transportation Management and SAP Note 2976757 - SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit: Central Note for Transfer Data Directly from SAP System - SAP EWM to EWM based on SAP S/4HANA.

What is the difference between LE-WM and Stock Room Management?

For our customers who are currently operating their warehouses with LE-WM, the following aspects are important to consider as transformation strategy boundary conditions:

  • LE-WM running on SAP ERP - please review the maintenance plan for your operative SAP ERP release via the SAP Product Availability Matrix.
  • LE-WM running on SAP S/4HANA - LE-WM is running in the compatibility scope with restricted usage rights, that expire according to general Compatibility Scope guidelines. Please review SAP Note 2269324 - Compatibility Scope Matrix for SAP S/4HANA for further information.
  • LE-WM running on SAP S/4HANA under RISE agreement there is an extended restricted usage rights period defined. Please review SAP Note 2269324 - Compatibility Scope Matrix for SAP S/4HANA for further information.

Stock Room Management is offering a subset of LE-WM functionalities. The offering is intended to enable our installed base customers to continue running their legacy  Warehouse Management installations in SAP S/4HANA beyond the timelines of the compatibility scope.

Please review the following SAP Notes to learn more about Stock Room Management:

  • SAP Note 2881166 - FAQ : Stock Room Management
  • SAP Note 2882809 - Scope Compliance Check for Stock Room Management
  • SAP Note 2577428 - Roadmap for LE-WM in S/4HANA

We would like to recommend the following SAP Blogs for further information around the topic of strategy, product versions and more:

  • SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 01 - History of SAP Extended Warehouse Management
  • Transformation to SAP S/4HANA for Warehouse and Transportation Management
  • Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030

entry.title: Re: Business Application Studio in BTP - Unable to connect to ABAP backend
entry.content:
Hi,
What is the exact error that you get when trying to access the ABAP service metadata through your dev space?
Did you try also accessing the same service metadata directly in your local network (with the ABAP system URL directly - not through any destination) with the same user credentials?
Since connectivity to the system works with other APIs (as fetching service list), it could be something with the specific service, or with the user permissions to access this service.

Thanks,
Rotem

entry.title: Re: Needed report - on G/L accounts that have been renamed -
entry.content: You can find App Id in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Librar  : G/L Account Changes for General Ledger Accountant for example.

entry.title: Batch Generation at the Time of Goods Receipt (Batch Management at the Material Level)
entry.content:
In the material master, there are two fields: Batch Management and Batch Management (Plant).
If only Batch Management is checked, and Batch Management (Plant) is not, then you cannot specify a batch when trying to post a goods receipt.

Why is that?

entry.title: Re: Best Practices for Onboarding and MiniEC – With a Focus on Offboarding and Crossboarding
entry.content:
Hi Fabian,

yes PM&GM works with mini EC. All data needed for PM&GM process will have to be replicated in EC and then synchronized in EP. EP will be fed only by EC and some data become read only in UI and therefore hris sync EC-EP must be correctly configured.

Thank you

Barbara

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client

Is this feature only available from a certain version?

Thanks,
Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!!
entry.content: The provided link for SAP Business AI - Roadmap Explorer doesn't work for partners. Any alternative for this?

entry.title: Re: Suppliers shouldn't be able to enter negative values...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Yes, you can prevent this phenomenom by making sure that the configuration for the document type does not allow the negative amount for the credit memo documents.

Here is where you can find it :

And then untick the negative values permission's option into the concerned document type :

I hope this will help solve your issue.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Is there any API in sap public cloud to read the full...
entry.content:
Hi Bhawesh,

I already use this "Journal Entry Item - Read (A2X)" API but it does not provides the full JE XML document data.

I need an API that supports viewing the full JE document XML data (via GET/POST REST commands) of existing JE documents created manually by the customer with tax information whose XML data contains complex tax XML tags (like <Tax> and <ProductTaxItem>) that I cannot access via  "Journal Entry Item - Read (A2X)" API.

If you know a way to download the full XML of an existing JE document, please let me known.

Regards

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content: Hi @thomasmakkink you need to implement https://me.sap.com/notes/3579012. Regards, Patrick

entry.title: How to handle generic JSON nodes with XCO?
entry.content:
We are calling a service where where we can get get varying JSON payload responses, and then processing the response with the XCO library (xco_cp_json=>data->from_string).

The outer part is common, but the inside can be different. For example:

{
  "data": {
    "items": [ 
      {
       // At this level, there are different fields depending on the call
      }
    ]
  }
}

I would like to get access to the list/table of entities inside the "items" element and forward that to a method that will handle the specific data depending on the situation.

So I would like to define a structure type to handle this, e.g.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF json_payload,
             BEGIN OF data,
               items TYPE ???, "<- what to use here?
               [ OR ]
               items TYPE TABLE OF ???, "<- what to use here?
             END OF data,
           END OF json_payload.

I am sure that one of the previous JSON libraries I used (perhaps it was my own) allowed me to specify a TYPE REF to a JSON node (e.g. CL_JSON_NODE) and after the deserialization, that would contain a reference to that portion of the JSON document that could then be processed further.

Any ideas on how to handle this kind of situation with XCO?

entry.title: Re: Email Notifications in SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
entry.content:
@avb-SF 

Hi Asya,

Does this issue still exist? We analyzed it internally some time back but weren't able to reproduce it.

Best,

Moritz

entry.title: Posting attachment to Item Level via API
entry.content:
Good day,

We are looking to post .pdf attachments to the item level of a Purchase Requisition, currently we are using the "odata/SAP/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet" API to perform the posting.

The issue we are experiencing, is that attachments are being posted to the header level.

Is there a method to post attachments to the item level?

entry.title: Re: Only Active records when using Termination
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 

Did some digging into the configuration and apparently, the Employment Status Dimension was different from what is in the Manual Implementation guide. It was like this, with the following SQL code: 

SELECT EMPLOYMENT_STATUS.EXTERNAL_CODE, EMPLOYMENT_STATUS.LABEL

FROM [%PICKLIST(PICKLIST__EMP_JOB_INFO_T.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS)%] EMPLOYMENT_STATUS

WHERE EMPLOYMENT_STATUS.LOCALE = 'en_GB'

After I changed it to what it looks like in the Manual Implementation Guide for Employment Status Dimension, it seems that the problem has been solved, as now I'm able to see employees with terminated status(see screenshot below). So for now, I believe this can be classified as the solution, at least for my configuration. Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Create Outbound Delivery in EWM in S/4HANA Cloud, Private - Best Approach
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are planning to develop a custom application (likely Fiori/UI5) on SAP BTP for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition system.

The application needs to allow users to create a Outbound Delivery from PM Order or via a direct Material Search.

We haven't found a standard public OData API that seems to cover creation of Outbound Delivery Order in EWM.

Given we are on S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, what is the recommended technical approach to build the backend integration for this custom application?

We appreciate any guidance, best practices, or pointers to relevant resources for developing custom applications in SAP BTP with backend integration in an S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition environment.

EWM - Warehouse Order Processing 

SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: Re: An error has occurred system message in GRC NWBC port...
entry.content:
Hi,

We got since April 15th the same issue with some users. Do you have any root cause already? thanks in advanced.

entry.title: Re: PO header field 'Your Reference EKKO-IHREZ' get changed with value ".." (2 dots) autom
entry.content: Look like a custom development if only custom fields are updated, so Abaper added a dummy update of some standard text field, and reset it in a second call?

entry.title: SAP IAS - Can Application Custom Attributes Be Used in Provisioning to the Target System
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

We are currently setting up user provisioning between SAP IAS as the source system and SAP Ariba as the target system, using SAP Identity Provisioning Service.

My question is about the Application Custom Attributes, which can be found in IAS under:
Users & Authorizations > User Management > [Select a User] > Applications.

Can these Application Custom Attributes (1–5) be used in the target system transformation mapping during provisioning?

We would like to use the values of these Application Custom Attributes in our IPS transformation mapping to populate fields in the target system (Ariba).

If it's not possible to use the Application Custom Attributes during the Provisioning, what are they used for?

Any clarification or best practice from the community would be highly appreciated!

 

Best regards 

Maurice

entry.title: Re: Big memory consumption XSLT, why Heap is used? Best w...
entry.content: Thanks. Yes, ST is about 10 times faster than XSLT (rule of thumb). XSLT should be used only for stuff which cannot be done via ST. My question seems to be the first one giving information about the memory used by XSLT versus ST. But my question is more about Heap and memory in general, I'd like to understand why Heap is used although EM is only few hundreds of MB and no other task in the system.

entry.title: Re: Sequential Multicast suppressing error details
entry.content:
Hi Ricardo,

Unfortunately, this do no work either. The problem we have is that Multicast is suppressing the error before sending it to the Exception Handler.

As we can see in this screenshot, the error happens within a Sequential Multicast. The body of the response is what I want to be able to read:

However, once we get into the Exception Handling, we do not have access to the org.apache.camel.component.ahc.AhcOperationFailedException, as expected in the your Groovy. Instead we get org.apache.camel.CamelExchangeException: Multicast processing failed for number 0.

This CamelExchangeException does not have the same detail within it.

If this call was not within a Multicast (unfortunately, it has to be) then your solution would be perfect. But Multicast is suppressing the error details and this is what I am trying to figure my way around.

Thank you,

Eoin

entry.title: Re: Configuration of Multiple VAT Rates in Sales Orders i...
entry.content: As long as you have two condition types for tax in your pricing procedure, and proper condition record is in place for different combination, yes, system will consider different tax percent in sales order but for different line items.  One line item or material cannot have two tax percentage.

entry.title: Re: Input type number in combination with binding type gi...
entry.content:
Hi Farooq,

Please note that according to our Rules of Engagement. "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content." We have edited your answer and added the GenAI-statement as we detected AI-generated content in your post. Thank you for your contribution in the SAP Community!

Best Regards

Kavya

entry.title: SAP HANA SDI real-time change data capture with HanaAdapterV2 does not replicate an UPDATE row
entry.content:
Hi,

We are replicating data from cloud-based S/4HANA system to on-premises HANA database. Typically a table gets an INSERT and within a second an UPDATE. The INSERT row is transferred successfully but the UPDATE gets lost.

  1. The adapter is HanaAdapterV2
  2. HANA on-premises is 2.00.079.01
  3. DP Agent is 2.7.6.1
  4. There are no error messages
  5. Most of the Remote Source parameters are left as default
  6. Both the INSERT and UPDATE are executed as UPSERT at the target database.

I have activated all possible tracing options but still fail to get meaningful information from tracefiles. The SQL trace on the target HANA database shows that the UPSERT command does not get executed at all (by the APPLIER) and gets lost in the earlier stages of the replication flow.

Please advise,
What are the ways to debug this problem?
What tracing options must get activated in order to see the actual data in various replication stages?
Should we use HanaAdapter instead of HanaAdapterV2?

Thank you in advance,
Jaak

entry.title: Re: refresh the system with Data and take the Configuration
entry.content: The answer can be found in note https://me.sap.com/notes/3401017/E

entry.title: Re: PO header field 'Your Reference EKKO-IHREZ' get chang...
entry.content:
Hi together,
we faced the same problem, after our S4 upgrade.
The reason was a job, that was executing program /SAPSLL/MM0A_OBJSSF_PROCESS_R3.
And this program has additional coding after the upgrade. That was causing the ".." change entries in
the purchase orders. But even worser, due to the change, the purchase orders got send out again to the supplier....
However:
Here the OSS note:
https://me.sap.com/notes/3479709/E

Here the note to fix it:
Hinweise - SAP for Me

Best regards
Jörg

entry.title: Re: Request for the CodeJam in Tbilisi, end of May, SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications: ML, Vectors,
entry.content:
Thank you. We have received your request and it is now in our planning board. The topic expert will be in touch soon.

Best regards,

Eberechukwu

entry.title: Re: Batch Generation at the Time of Goods Receipt (Batch...
entry.content:
Hello @aoba 

If only Batch Management is checked, and Batch Management (Plant) is not, then the material is not batch managed in the plant. Thus, you can't specify a batch during goods receipt.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Upload Project Stock (WBS) at MRP Area level and PIR consume.
entry.content: Hi SandeepNokia, Have you find any solutin to this problems? I have the same scenario...

entry.title: Re: Alumni Experience- 1H2025 Release
entry.content: @Gopinath Does it require EC license in order to use Alumni feature? (apart from ONB license)

entry.title: Re: Getting error while executing FAGLGVTR
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

This issue relates to Asset Accounting (FI-AA) and the fiscal year change procedure in SAP S/4HANA - specifically when running transaction FAGLGVTR (Carry Forward Balances), you’re receiving an error because more than two fiscal years are open in Asset Accounting for company code 1000.

 

Diagnosis of the error

SAP only allows maximum two open fiscal years in Asset Accounting at a time (e.g., 2023 and 2024). But currently:

  • The system still considers 2023 as open
  • You want to carry forward to 2025, which would make three open years: 2023, 2024, and 2025
  • This triggers the error message during the General Ledger carryforward

 

Solution Steps

To proceed with FAGLGVTR, you must close the fiscal year 2023 in Asset Accounting using:

Transaction: AJAB – Year-End Closing for Asset Accounting

Steps to Close Asset Fiscal Year (AJAB):

  1. Go to transaction AJAB
  2. Enter company code: 1000
  3. Enter fiscal year: 2023
  4. Execute
  5. Review any error logs (such as assets not fully depreciated, incomplete data, or pending transactions)
  6. Resolve all outstanding issues
  7. Rerun AJAB to close the year

Once year 2023 is closed in Asset Accounting, the “Highest Fiscal Year” will automatically update, and the system will allow carryforward to 2025.

 

Why you cannot manually change “Highest Fiscal Year” in FAA_CMP

The field is system-controlled — you cannot manually update it unless:

  • You reset the year-end close (AJRW) and reclose it
  • Or you programmatically intervene

 

After AJAB Is Done

Then you can proceed to:

Run FAGLGVTR for G/L Balance Carryforward to 2025

 

I hope this will help solve your issue.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: NOT ABLE TO POST PO FOR NLAG MATERIALS
entry.content:
hello jagdeepsingh,

 

Thank you for the reply, I tried to use acc assignment  as 'P', even though it is showing error we have used same material previously even not updating value and quantity basis.

Kindly guide on this .

entry.title: Re: Determine SAP UI5 Fiori runtime context in core ABAP...
entry.content:
You can use the FM SYSTEM_CALLSTAK to establish if your code has been called via an OData call. In my system, it starts with class /IWBEP/CL_MGW_REMOTE_HANDLER, but you should check if that's the same in your system.

CONSTANTS c_odata_prog TYPE SYREPID VALUE '/IWBEP/CL_MGW_REMOTE_HANDLER==CP'.
DATA callstack_table TYPE ABAP_CALLSTACK.

CALL FUNCTION 'SYSTEM_CALLSTACK'
 IMPORTING
   CALLSTACK = callstack_table.

LOOP AT callstack_table INTO DATA(callstack_entry).
  IF callstack_entry-mainprogram = c_odata_prog.
    "this is an OData call
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

entry.title: New Machine Learning and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q1
entry.content:
With the SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q1 release, several new embedded Machine Learning / AI functions have been released with the SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL). Key new capabilities to be highlighted are introduction of text analysis, text vectorization, vector data as input to many more Machine Learning functions like classification and regression in the SAP HANA Cloud database. An enhancement summary is available in the What’s new document for SAP HANA Cloud database 2025.02 (QRC 1/2025).

Text extraction and text search enhancements

Text extraction from binary files

A new Document Filter Library (DFL) has been released, which provides functions to extract plain text from binary files like PDFs stored as BLOBs in SAP HANA Cloud tables. By use of the DFL_EXTRACT_TEXT_FROM_DOCUMENTS procedure, application developers and data scientist are enabled to unlock the valuable insights from text hidden in those files.

Extracted text is made available for text preprocessing tasks like chunking, NLP (e.g. entity extraction, sentiment detection), text vectorization using SAP HANA Cloud embedding models and further downstream analysis tasks based on text vectors such as similarity search, text mining, clustering and classification.

This enhancement not only widens the scope of your data analysis but also makes it easier to leverage text-based insights in your applications.

The Document Filter Library (DFL) is provided as a SAP HANA Cloud plugin. The setup instructions are documented in the SAP HANA Cloud administration guide and include the following steps

  • the plugin needs to be installed and Script Server activated
  • SQL user privileges need to be granted

Further note, the document filter library in SAP HANA Cloud provides comparable text from binary files extraction-capabilities like the text analysis and full text indexing from binary files features in SAP HANA Platform, for respective reference see the text analysis SQL API for extraction of text from binary documents in the Text Analysis Developer Guide or the SAP HANA search developer guide for document mime-type reference.

 

Keyword-based text search enhancements

Information retrieval from text typically starts with text search techniques. The newly released keyword-based search function BM25 search (SEARCH_DOCS_BY_KEYWORDS) has been further enhanced to support complete natural sentences as input to the BM25 search:

 

Classification, regression, time series and AutoML model enhancements

Hybrid Gradient Boosting Trees (HGBT) enhancements

HGBT now supports multi-target regression and multi-label classification models, alike MT_MLP modelling predictions for multiple columns using a single model.

  • Multiple DEPENDENT_VARIABLE values can be set as parameters to enable this
  • In addition, REAL_VECTOR columns are supported as target, and a multiple target regression model applied.
  • The objective functions supported for multi-target/label models
    • Regression OBJ_FUNC = 0 (Squared error)
    • Classification OBJ_FUNC = 7 (Softmax)
  • Using the new parameter USE_VEC_LEAF, allows to control for each tree leaf to have a vector-value as a predictor (default is no)

As the prediction output incl. values for multiple targets or classes, a new function  PAL_HGBT_MULTI_TASK_PREDICT is introduced generating the extended output

 

HGBT regression models with trend extrapolation for Time Series

Tree-based models like Hybrid Gradient Boosting Trees are great at capturing patterns from the training data. However, they falter when it comes to projecting these patterns into the future, hence especially when models like HGBT regressors are applied in time series forecasting. A regular such model has no means to infer trends or patterns beyond the bounds of the training data, making true extrapolation impossible. For a detailed discussion see for example this blog post: overcoming the limitations of tree-based models in time series forecasting.

HGBTs, now introduces a linear component to the tree-building process, allowing the model to capture linear patterns and thus trend extrapolation capabilities

  • New parameter MODEL_TREE, when setting it to linear, the model will try to fit a linear model at each leaf node.

With this key enhancement PAL Hybrid Gradient Boosting Tree (HGBT) now allows for building superior regression models, with improved trend extrapolation of value outside of the training data scope. 

 

Unified classification / regression enhancements

The modernized and much enhanced Multi-task MLP Neural Network modeling function, can now be used with the Unified Classification/Regression function

  • This unlocks local explainability when using Multi-task MLP models, the predictions providing reason codes with Shapley explanations (using parameters BACKGROUND_SIZE, BACKGROUND_SAMPLING_SEED)
  • However in the current update, the models are constrained a single target/label only when used via Unified Classification/Regression

 

AutoML for time series optimization enhancements

AutoML introduces an improved hyperparameter optimization for time series models

  • random-search optimization is now utilizing Hyperband
    • A new parameter WITH_HYPERBAND enables the enhancement in conjunction with SEARCH_METHOD set as "random"
    • The optimization now more efficiently allocates resources to different hyperparameter configurations, and significantly speeds up the optimization process

 

Python ML client (hana-ml) enhancements

The full list of new methods and enhancements with hana_ml 2.24  is summarized in the changelog for hana-ml 2.24 as part of the documentation. The key enhancements in this release include

Dataframe enhancements

  • New Vector- / vector-index management methods
  • New sort by vector-similarity

AutoML and pipeline modeling improvements

  • Data-parallel/massive AutoML
    progress monitor enhanced
  • New AutoML regression
    outlier detection

Text processing enhancements

  • New Text Analysis, POS, NER, Sentiment Analysis
  • Enhanced BM25 search by doc, sentence

Further misc. enhancements

  • New Tree Debriefing to text for decision tree
  • Enhanced CAP generation for AdditiveModelForecast, AutoML
    and Unified APIs wrt use of input/output signatures

You can find an examples notebook illustrating the highlighted feature enhancements here 25QRC01_2.24.ipynb. 

 

Generative AI-toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud (hana-ai)

Introduction

The new generative AI-toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud (hana-ai) is an extension of the existing Python ML client for SAP HANA (hana-ml), mainly focusing on generative AI-assisted machine learning scenario development using hana-ml, thus streamlining embedding of ML capabilities with SAP BTP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) apps.

It builds upon many leading-edge generative AI related open source Python libraries (e.g. langchain) and provides seamless integration with SAP HANA Cloud, HANA vector engine, and others Python libraries like SAP GenAI Hub SDK. 

Key capabilities for AI-assisted HANA ML development and code generation

  • Use code-template knowledge stores and new hana-ml tools
    • Tools for code generation as well as executable tools for agentic-use of HANA ML for Forecasting
  • Conversational agent for HANA ML
    • Generative AI agent, utilizing executable tools targeted for HANA ML
    • Unlocking agentic, AI-assisted HANA ML development
  • Further conversational interfaces and agents
    • HANA dataframe agent for invoke code template-based tasks using hana-ml in python
    • SQL agent for invoking code template-based and langchain-db tool tasks via SQL generation and execution
    • Conversational SmartDataFrame interface, for applying standard dataframe methods as well as code template-based tasks to a dataframe

Highlighted features

Using sample code knowledge stores

  • Leverage hana-ml samples as knowledge store
    • Python | SQL default samples for PAL functions
    • Can be augmented with additional content, created with custom / best practice templates

 

Using new set of agent-tools targeted for simplified use of HANA ML

  • Library of executable hana-ml tools
    • Tools execute independently and generate results, beyond code generation
    • Initial set of tools focused on Time Series Forecasting
    • Time series analysis tools applied to the time series data,
      determining guidance about which time series algorithms to apply
    • Can be augmented with additional, custom created tools
    • Utilized for automated and agentic-, generative AI-assisted
      HANA ML development or via direct tool-usage

 

Using new set of agent-tools targeted for simplified use of HANA ML

  • Library of executable hana-ml tools
    • Tools execute independently and generate results, beyond code generation

Using a conversational agent for HANA ML

  • New Agent for HANA ML
    • Ready to use agent to build HANA Cloud PAL forecast models
    • Conversational agent session incl. chat history, based on langchain
    • Leverages executable tools specifically targeted for HANA ML
    • HANA ML-tools: initially focused on Time Series Forecasting like
      automatic time series data analysis, forecast algorithm proposal
      and fitting, or CAP project artifact generation

 

The new generative AI-toolkit unlocks and simplifies embedded AI SAP BTP application development with natural language assistance providing a faster getting-started experience, automated code generation based on template code samples, hana-ml tools for an conversational agent.

For further details see the Introduction at github.com/SAP/generative-ai-toolkit-for-sap-hana-cloud and the documentation at sap.github.io/generative-ai-toolkit-for-sap-hana-cloud.

A sample python notebook using the conversational agent can be found at github.com/SAP-samples/hana-ml-samples/.../Generative-AI-toolkit-SAPHANACloud-Demo-SalesRefunds-Forecasting

Setup instruction for generative AI-toolkit (hana-ai)

  • Download the hana-ai package from github releases and install it using _pip install generative-ai-toolkit-for-sap-hana-cloud-x.x.x.zip_, or use _pip install hana-ai_ for install from pypi.org/hana-ai.

  • Setup of the SAP generative AI hub SDK
    • Install the required Python packages pip install "generative-ai-hub-sdk[all]".
    • Create a deployment for a generative AI model in the SAP BTP, for instructions see the SAP AI core documentation section Create a Deployment for a Generative AI Model.
    • The generative AI hub SDK reuses configuration settings from the AI-core-SDK, these include client ID, client secret, authentication URL, base URL, and resource group. You can set these values as environment variables or via a config file. For detailed instructions see this SAP Learning unit on using Generative-AI-Hub-SDK.

 Overall, again a great set of Machine Learning and AI capabilities enhancements with this SAP HANA Cloud release!

entry.title: Which signavio best practices apply to plant maintenance without phase model
entry.content:
Team 

We have best practices for Phase model 4HH etc but which best practices apply for customers using classical model in private cloud.

While going through bh1 corrective maint we don't see the fiori apps such as maintenance planning overview and perform maint jobs in test scripts .  Wanted to know which best practice scenario can be used to accurately demo a classical model approach using latest fiori approach.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Russia bank account number validations
entry.content: Look at OSS for notes such as 2989899 - Russia: Bank accounts of Federal Reserve and Treasury Banks reported as invalid.

entry.title: Re: Object Definition not coming in Manage Data
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your query. In addition, it is advised to check below:

1. KBA2565939 - How to set permissions to MDF objects

https://me.sap.com/notes/2565939/E

2. Handbook: Implementing the Metadata Framework (MDF) >> Permission https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/implementing-metadata-framework-mdf/permissions?q=manage+data

 

If the issue still exists after checking all the necessary permissions, please raise an incident to LOD-SF-MDF via Sap For Me, MDF colleagues will reach out to you and porivide possible workaround.

 

Best regards,

Hannah

entry.title: How to display values based on lead selection in RAP?
entry.content: If i have two tables, lead selecting some entries in first table should display values in my second table according to the lead selected values.

entry.title: Re: Out-of-the-Box Document Grounding in SAP AI Core: RAG with SAP Help Portal
entry.content: Hi @Cocquerel is your SAP AI Core instance with an extended plan?

entry.title: Cost center budgeting for eam order
entry.content:
Team

We have cost center budgeting but that doesn't seem to be integrated with eam order for active availability check. Is there any feature coming in future scope 

Thanks

Gaurav

entry.title: Re: How to use Support User after enabling single-sign on
entry.content: When adding a user in SLD under Users, you have an option to use Active Directory or SAP B1 Authentication server. Use B1 AS to add users that are authenticated directly in B1 (like manager or Support).

entry.title: Re: Retention Period of PDF Attachments (on Journal Entri...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, attachments such as PDF documents added to Journal Entries or Supplier Invoices are stored within SAP’s Secure File Storage and are fully auditable and retrievable - provided they are within the SAP-defined data retention period and not explicitly deleted by an admin.

 

Key Points – Retention of PDF Attachments in S/4HANA Cloud Public

1. Where Are Attachments Stored?

  • Attachments (PDFs, Excel, etc.) are stored using SAP’s content repository service integrated into the system.
  • This service ensures compliance with audit and security standards, including GDPR and ISO certifications.

2. Default Retention Policy

  • There is no automatic deletion of standard attachments (e.g., PDFs on Journal Entries or Invoices).
  • Documents and their attachments remain in the system as long as the business documents exist.
  • SAP retains data based on standard retention rules, typically 10+ years, depending on your country-specific legal requirements (e.g., in Switzerland, 10 years for accounting documents).

3. Access for Auditors

  • Auditors can retrieve attachments directly from the system (if they have access) using apps like:
    • Display Journal Entries
    • Manage Supplier Invoices
    • Display Line Items in General Ledger
  • No need to print the documents if audit access is granted digitally.

4. Is Printing Required?

  • Not required for legal compliance — provided the audit is supported electronically and the system’s integrity is maintained.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud is designed for paperless compliance, and attachments are considered legally valid evidence.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Connect & Exchange Corner on SAP Learning Hub, student edition
entry.content:
Hello Sibel,

this sign-up link was used by professors. I shared them via e-mail for Early Adopters. Do you know who I can contact in terms of registration support needs?

entry.title: sort table by custom added column
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

in a table i have for columns a measure, GL accounts and cross calculation.

i added a column "gross margin $ " and i want to sort the table based on it's values.

how can i achive this? it's not availble in the sort options

the end goal is to have the table sorted by the "gross margin $" calculation

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
Hello Sandra_Rossi,

I got it. Regarding the dump, I'm going to search an OSS note. Indeed, when the call transaction is launched in batch=X, I have a dump while the program provides for this batch = X.

Regarding the herearchies of screens, I honestly didn't know this option. There she is:


The problem is that when I view my CU50 report, I then have to click on the indicated button of an arrow to display the table I want. The question is whether it is possible to retrieve these values after my call transaction (so without clicking on this button...)

Costgrid is the table 1 and I want to retrieve data of table 2:

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Re: Calculate yearly absences pro-ratio for Compensation Planning
entry.content: Well written and very interesting blog! Thank you

entry.title: Re: Empty batch in vl10c->background
entry.content: Select "User Role" tab from vl10c, click on "Role" and see what is displaying against "Select rule".  I presume, it would have been showing "1" there.  Change it to 2 or 3

entry.title: Re: credit account has not been defined
entry.content: In GL Account determination, try setting up expense and purchase credit accounts. For some unknown reasons, these accounts need to be set in order to use payment documents.

entry.title: Re: Custom fields are not displayed in the system-generat...
entry.content: Dear Sahil,

entry.title: Re: Connect & Exchange Corner on SAP Learning Hub, student edition
entry.content:
How to verify your Learning Hub Student Edition?

Hello colleagues, I am now testing the registration to Student Edition with several professors. How can they verify that the Student Edition license was activated? 
I advised them to go to Subscriptions in Learning Hub. But there they have the old Academic Edition.
How does this work - do Student Edition replace Academic Edition Subscription?
Or if you have any manual for that - please share with me, thanks.

entry.title: Re: Seamless Planning with Datasphere & SAC: Seamless Planning in the Cloud?
entry.content: Eine deutsche Version des Beitrags findet sich unter https://beratungscontor.de/news/blogartikel/seamless-planning/

entry.title: Re: PDF Sending to REST API with JavaScript in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi @Keerthana,
I use APIM to avoid the CORS issue. The request should be proceed like below:
SAP Build App -> SAP API Management -> SAP Cloud Integration Iflow -> Document Information Extraction.

Hope this will help!

entry.title: Situation handling in eam
entry.content:
Team

Do we need a licence to use situation handling and is this a configurable product by functional consultants or do we need technical expertise to configure scenarios e.g if too many work orders are pending release for x days we need alerts etc?

Are there existing std eam available situation handling scenarios in the roadmap to implement as standard product  thanks

entry.title: Re: Requirement class for sales documents (Details)
entry.content: Did you check transaction OVZG ?

entry.title: Re: Updated HRIS Mappings - but HRIS Sync job not updatin...
